lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਣੁ ਗੁਰ ਪੀਰੈ ਕੋ ਥਾਇ ਨ ਪਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: vin gur peerai ko thaae na paaee |
lines.english: Without the Guru or a spiritual teacher, no one is accepted.
They may be shown the way, but only a few go there.
Without the karma of good actions, heaven is not attained.
Wearing ear-rings, they wander around the world.
The Way of Yoga is demonstrated in the Yogi's monastery.
They wear ear-rings to show the way.
The Creator Lord is everywhere.
One who knows the Lord here, realizes Him there as well.
When one's death warrant is issued, there is no delay.
There are as many travellers as there are beings.
without the karma of good actions, no one crosses over.
One who speaks the True Name of the True Lord,
Everyone's account is read in the Court of the Lord;
Others, whether Hindu or Muslim, are just babbling.
O Nanak, is not called to account hereafter. ||2||
Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਕਾ ਮੰਦਰੁ ਆਖੀਐ ਕਾਇਆ ਕੋਟੁ ਗੜੁ ॥
lines.translit: har kaa mandar aakheeai kaaeaa kott garr |
lines.english: The fortress of the body is called the Mansion of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਕਾ ਮੰਦਰੁ ਸਰੀਰੁ ਅਤਿ ਸੋਹਣਾ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਦਿੜੁ ॥
lines.translit: har kaa mandar sareer at sohanaa har har naam dirr |
lines.english: The body, the Mansion of the Lord, is very beautiful, when the Name of the Lord, Har, Har, is implanted deep within.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਦਰਿ ਲਾਲ ਜਵੇਹਰੀ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਪੜੁ ॥
lines.translit: andar laal javeharee guramukh har naam parr |
lines.english: The rubies and gems are found within it; the Gurmukh chants the Name of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖ ਆਪਿ ਖੁਆਇਅਨੁ ਮਾਇਆ ਮੋਹ ਨਿਤ ਕੜੁ ॥
lines.translit: manamukh aap khuaaeian maaeaa moh nit karr |
lines.english: The self-willed manmukhs ruin themselves; they boil continuously in attachment to Maya.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭਨਾ ਸਾਹਿਬੁ ਏਕੁ ਹੈ ਪੂਰੈ ਭਾਗਿ ਪਾਇਆ ਜਾਈ ॥੧੧॥
lines.translit: sabhanaa saahib ek hai poorai bhaag paaeaa jaaee |11|
lines.english: The One Lord is the Master of all. He is found only by perfect destiny. ||11||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਸਤਿ ਮੂੰਡ ਮੁਡਾਈ ਕੇਸੀ ਨਾ ਸਤਿ ਪੜਿਆ ਦੇਸ ਫਿਰਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: naa sat moondd muddaaee kesee naa sat parriaa des fireh |
lines.english: There is no Truth in shaving one's head; there is no Truth is studying the scriptures or wandering in foreign lands.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਸਤਿ ਦੁਖੀਆ ਨਾ ਸਤਿ ਸੁਖੀਆ ਨਾ ਸਤਿ ਪਾਣੀ ਜੰਤ ਫਿਰਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: naa sat dukheea naa sat sukheea naa sat paanee jant fireh |
lines.english: There is no Truth in suffering, there is no Truth in comfort. There is no Truth in wandering like animals through the water.
Salok, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਸਤਿ ਰੁਖੀ ਬਿਰਖੀ ਪਥਰ ਆਪੁ ਤਛਾਵਹਿ ਦੁਖ ਸਹਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: naa sat rukhee birakhee pathar aap tachhaaveh dukh saheh |
lines.english: There is no Truth in trees, plants or stones, in mutilating oneself or suffering in pain.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਸਤਿ ਹਸਤੀ ਬਧੇ ਸੰਗਲ ਨਾ ਸਤਿ ਗਾਈ ਘਾਹੁ ਚਰਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: naa sat hasatee badhe sangal naa sat gaaee ghaahu chareh |
lines.english: There is no Truth in binding elephants in chains; there is no Truth in grazing cows.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਹਥਿ ਸਿਧਿ ਦੇਵੈ ਜੇ ਸੋਈ ਜਿਸ ਨੋ ਦੇਇ ਤਿਸੁ ਆਇ ਮਿਲੈ ॥
lines.translit: jis hath sidh devai je soee jis no dee tis aae milai |
lines.english: He alone grants it, whose hands hold spritual perfection; he alone receives it, unto whom it is given.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭਿ ਘਟ ਮੇਰੇ ਹਉ ਸਭਨਾ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਜਿਸਹਿ ਖੁਆਈ ਤਿਸੁ ਕਉਣੁ ਕਹੈ ॥
lines.translit: sabh ghatt mere hau sabhanaa andar jiseh khuaaee tis kaun kahai |
lines.english: God says, all hearts are mine, and I am in all hearts. Who can explain this to one who is confused?

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਤਾ ਕਉ ਮਿਲੈ ਵਡਾਈ ਜਿਸੁ ਘਟ ਭੀਤਰਿ ਸਬਦੁ ਰਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: naanak taa kau milai vaddaaee jis ghatt bheetar sabad ravai |
lines.english: O Nanak, he alone is blessed with glorious greatness, whose heart is filled with the Word of the Shabad.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸਹਿ ਭੁਲਾਈ ਪੰਧ ਸਿਰਿ ਤਿਸਹਿ ਦਿਖਾਵੈ ਕਉਣੁ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: jiseh bhulaaee pandh sir tiseh dikhaavai kaun |1|
lines.english: And who can show the Path to that being whom I have confused since the beginning of time? ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸਹਿ ਦਿਖਾਲਾ ਵਾਟੜੀ ਤਿਸਹਿ ਭੁਲਾਵੈ ਕਉਣੁ ॥
lines.translit: jiseh dikhaalaa vaattarree tiseh bhulaavai kaun |
lines.english: Who can confuse that being, unto whom I have shown the Way?
He alone is a householder, who restrains his passions
First Mehl:
and begs for meditation, austerity and self-discipline.
such a householder is as pure as the water of the Ganges.
He gives donations to charity with his body;

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਰਮ ਤੰਤ ਮਹਿ ਰੇਖ ਨ ਰੂਪੁ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: param tant meh rekh na roop |2|
lines.english: The supreme essence of reality has no shape or form. ||2||
Says Eeshar, the Lord is the embodiment of Truth.
He begs for suffering as his food.
He alone is a detached hermit, who burns away his self-conceit.
First Mehl:
In the city of the heart, he begs for charity.
Says Gorakh, God is the embodiment of Truth;
Such a renunciate ascends to the City of God.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਰਮ ਤੰਤ ਮਹਿ ਰੇਖ ਨ ਰੂਪੁ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: param tant meh rekh na roop |3|
lines.english: the supreme essence of reality has no shape or form. ||3||
He balances the sun and the moon energies.
First Mehl:
He sees the Immaculate Lord dwelling in both the upper and lower regions.
He alone is an Udasi, a shaven-headed renunciate, who embraces renunciation.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਰਮ ਤੰਤ ਮਹਿ ਰੇਖ ਨ ਰੂਪੁ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: param tant meh rekh na roop |4|
lines.english: the supreme essence of reality has no shape or form. ||4||
Says Gopi Chand, God is the embodiment of Truth;

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸੁ ਉਦਾਸੀ ਕਾ ਪੜੈ ਨ ਕੰਧੁ ॥
lines.translit: tis udaasee kaa parrai na kandh |
lines.english: The body-wall of such an Udasi does not collapse.
First Mehl:
The fire of his body illuminates God within.
He does not waste his energy in wet dreams.
He alone is a Paakhandi, who cleanses his body of filth.
